verses.text: He goes to a garden; Judas comes with a band, 'lanterns and torches and weapons.' Armed against the Lamb. They come in force against the willing Saviour.
He goes to a garden; Judas comes with a band, 'lanterns and torches and weapons.' Armed against the Lamb. They come in force against the willing Saviour.
He goes to a garden; Judas comes with a band, 'lanterns and torches and weapons.' Armed against the Lamb. They come in force against the willing Saviour.
Whom seek ye? 'I am he'; they go backward and fall to the ground. A flash of glory. One word lays His captors low.
Whom seek ye? 'I am he'; they go backward and fall to the ground. A flash of glory. One word lays His captors low.
Whom seek ye? 'I am he'; they go backward and fall to the ground. A flash of glory. One word lays His captors low.
'Let these go their way,' that none He was given be lost. Shielding love. He guards His own even in His arrest.
'Let these go their way,' that none He was given be lost. Shielding love. He guards His own even in His arrest.
'Let these go their way,' that none He was given be lost. Shielding love. He guards His own even in His arrest.
Peter smites off an ear; 'the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?' Willing cup. He accepts the suffering the Father appoints.
Peter smites off an ear; 'the cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?' Willing cup. He accepts the suffering the Father appoints.
They bind Him and lead Him to Annas; Caiaphas had said one should die. The plot moves. The councils' scheming runs its course.
They bind Him and lead Him to Annas; Caiaphas had said one should die. The plot moves. The councils' scheming runs its course.
They bind Him and lead Him to Annas; Caiaphas had said one should die. The plot moves. The councils' scheming runs its course.
Peter stands at the fire; 'Art not thou also one of his disciples? ... I am not.' First denial. By the enemy's fire he denies his Lord.
Peter stands at the fire; 'Art not thou also one of his disciples? ... I am not.' First denial. By the enemy's fire he denies his Lord.
Peter stands at the fire; 'Art not thou also one of his disciples? ... I am not.' First denial. By the enemy's fire he denies his Lord.
Peter stands at the fire; 'Art not thou also one of his disciples? ... I am not.' First denial. By the enemy's fire he denies his Lord.
Asked of His doctrine, 'I spake openly'; ask them that heard Me. Nothing hidden. His teaching needs no secret defence.
Asked of His doctrine, 'I spake openly'; ask them that heard Me. Nothing hidden. His teaching needs no secret defence.
Asked of His doctrine, 'I spake openly'; ask them that heard Me. Nothing hidden. His teaching needs no secret defence.
An officer strikes Him; 'if I have spoken evil, bear witness.' Patient under wrong. He answers blows with calm reason.
An officer strikes Him; 'if I have spoken evil, bear witness.' Patient under wrong. He answers blows with calm reason.
An officer strikes Him; 'if I have spoken evil, bear witness.' Patient under wrong. He answers blows with calm reason.
Peter again denies; 'Did not I see thee in the garden?' immediately the cock crew. Threefold fall. The warned disciple denies as foretold.
Peter again denies; 'Did not I see thee in the garden?' immediately the cock crew. Threefold fall. The warned disciple denies as foretold.
Peter again denies; 'Did not I see thee in the garden?' immediately the cock crew. Threefold fall. The warned disciple denies as foretold.
They bring Him to Pilate but enter not the hall; 'It is not lawful for us to put any man to death.' Scrupulous and cruel. They fear defilement while plotting murder.
They bring Him to Pilate but enter not the hall; 'It is not lawful for us to put any man to death.' Scrupulous and cruel. They fear defilement while plotting murder.

They bring Him to Pilate but enter not the hall; 'It is not lawful for us to put any man to death.' Scrupulous and cruel. They fear defilement while plotting murder.
Art thou the King of the Jews? thine own nation delivered thee. The charge. Pilate probes the claim of kingship.
Art thou the King of the Jews? thine own nation delivered thee. The charge. Pilate probes the claim of kingship.
Art thou the King of the Jews? thine own nation delivered thee. The charge. Pilate probes the claim of kingship.
'My kingdom is not of this world'; to bear witness to the truth was I born. A higher kingdom. His reign is of truth, not of force.
'My kingdom is not of this world'; to bear witness to the truth was I born. A higher kingdom. His reign is of truth, not of force.
'What is truth?' I find no fault; they cry for Barabbas, a robber. Truth unsought. Pilate asks but waits not for the answer.
